what_it_does: An acceptance test script that exercises the Suite chat() and chat_parts() refactor, verifying shared-core routing, distinct return shapes, mode-driven part grain, conditional staging, tools-only-on-final-part, monkeypatch seam integrity, once-per-part context assembly, drift-home documentation, and an optional live staged turn against a local 4B endpoint.
observations: The file is organized into numbered sections that map checks to design-doc criteria such as C4.1, C4.2, C4.3, C4.5.
It monkeypatches both fabric.client.complete_with_tools and the Suite instance method _model_supports_tools to verify both chat() and chat_parts() use the same seams.
It asserts three distinct return-shape key counts: off=4, refusal=5, normal=7.
It counts store events of kind cognition.wave and chat to verify once-only rollup and epilogue behavior.
It uses a global PASS counter incremented by a check() helper that asserts and prints per-label results.
It creates fresh Suite instances in temporary directories via fresh_suite() and reuses tmp path strings with suffixes.
It includes an optional live test against a hardcoded local endpoint (http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1) that is skipped-with-note if down.
It reads runtime/AGENTS.md to confirm THOUGHT_SHAPES and PART_GRAIN are documented as drift home.
It uses lambda assignments to instance attributes to force gate true/false and to force available_models to raise.
It restores the original fabric.client.complete_with_tools in try/finally blocks around each patched section.
standouts: The monkeypatch seam checks are the strongest signal: they independently verify that both chat() and chat_parts() honor the same instance gate and module-level complete_with_tools, catching a 'forked brain' bug.
Conditional-staging checks are mandatory and adversarial: trivial turns and never-stage modes (focus/background/off) must bypass the entire cognition machine and emit zero cognition.wave events.
The tools-on-final-part-only probe records the offered tool count per part call and asserts the first intermediate part gets zero tools while the final part gets a non-zero set.
The optional live by-use section turns the stubbed refactor proofs into an end-to-end check against a resident 4B model, but explicitly skips rather than falsely greening if the endpoint is down.
conventions: Acceptance test written as a top-level script with helper functions rather than a unittest/pytest class.
Each assertion uses a named check() helper that increments a global PASS counter and prints an 'ok <label>' line.
Monkeypatched module functions are saved and restored in try/finally blocks.
Criteria labels are embedded in assertion strings so failure messages name the design-doc requirement.
Live integration checks are guarded by endpoint_up() and report skip-with-note instead of failing.
concerns: The test relies heavily on monkeypatching internal implementation details (_model_supports_tools, available_models, fabric.client.complete_with_tools), making it brittle to internal refactors.
It asserts a specific warning summary string 'chat model registry unreachable' without showing that string is defined in this file, so the check may break if the warning text changes.
The live endpoint test hardcodes a model name and base URL that may not exist in other environments.
Return-shape distinctness is checked via key-count equality rather than explicit key sets for all shapes, and the off/refusal/normal contracts are encoded in comments more than data.
Temporary directory paths are concatenated with string suffixes (tmp + '/3') rather than using os.path.join consistently.
Global mutable state (PASS, sys.path, os.chdir) and broad try/finally cleanup could mask isolation issues between sections.
notes: A criteria-tagged acceptance test that uses stubbing and optional live inference to prove the G4 chat/chat_parts shared-core refactor behaves consistently across gating, staging, tooling, and history contracts.
questions: Where are _chat_prologue, _chat_part_core, and _chat_epilogue defined and what are their exact input/output contracts?
What is the full enumeration of Suite.MODES and the mapping in PART_GRAIN?
How does the run://<turn>/<role> resolver work and what G3 rule decides injection?
What is the expected schema of the cognition.wave event summary payload used in the live test?
What does the 'rhm_*' test family refer to and why is it insufficient for the forked-brain check?
How is the resident 4B endpoint expected to be launched and what model capabilities does it expose?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file content: it is an acceptance test for the G4 refactor and staged-response queue, exercising the listed criteria with both stubbed and optional live checks.
apparent_themes: Shared-core refactor with no duplicated control flow between chat() and chat_parts().
Conditional staging to avoid spinning up cognition for trivial or never-stage modes.
Seam integrity via monkeypatching to detect hidden forked-brain paths.
Deterministic stub tests plus optional live by-use validation.
Provenance asymmetry in chat history grading (gold user, working assistant).
Once-per-part resource assembly and once-only epilogue for staged turns.
Drift home: runtime documentation must mirror code-level shape/grain tables.
intention_signals: The file is trying to guarantee that chat() and chat_parts() are not two independent implementations, that staging and gating cannot be silently bypassed, and that tooling/grain/history contracts hold under both stubbed and live inference conditions.
novelty: It combines stub-driven acceptance criteria with an explicit monkeypatch seam test for both chat() and chat_parts(), and it includes an optional live local-4B by-use proof that skips-with-note rather than greening falsely.
contribution: A deterministic acceptance test that documents and verifies the G4 staged-response queue contracts, return-shape distinctions, shared-core seam behavior, once-per-part context assembly, and conditional-staging rules, with an optional live endpoint smoke test.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test script for the G4 staged-response queue that verifies the chat()/chat_parts() shared-core refactor, distinct return shapes, mode-driven grain, conditional staging, tools-on-final-part, monkeypatch seam integrity, once-per-part context, drift-home docs, and an optional live local-4B inference turn.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the G4 refactor and staging rules are honored by both chat() and chat_parts() under stubbed and optional live conditions, and to catch silent forked-brain or bypass bugs.

symbols.does: Asserts a labeled condition and increments the global PASS counter.
symbols.description: The central assertion helper used throughout the acceptance test. It takes a human-readable label and a boolean condition, asserts the condition with the label in the failure message, increments the global PASS counter, and prints an 'ok <label>' line.

symbols.does: Builds a minimal OpenAI-style tool_call dictionary for stub responses.
symbols.description: Returns a dictionary representing a function tool call with a fixed id 'call_1', type 'function', and the supplied name and JSON-encoded arguments. Used to construct stubbed complete_with_tools responses in the normal-path and seam tests.

symbols.does: Creates a new FsStore, NodeRegistry, and Suite in a temporary directory.
symbols.description: Initializes a fresh test fixture by constructing an FsStore at <tmp>/store, discovering nodes from the nodes directory, and returning the Suite and store. Each numbered section calls this to reduce cross-test state.

symbols.does: Counts recent store events whose kind is 'cognition.wave'.
symbols.description: Scans the store's recent_events(2000) and returns the number of events with kind equal to 'cognition.wave'. Used to verify that non-staging modes and trivial turns do not spin up the cognition swarm.

symbols.does: Counts recent store events whose kind is 'chat'.
symbols.description: Scans the store's recent_events(2000) and returns the number of events with kind equal to 'chat'. Used to verify the once-only epilogue behavior for off and live staged turns.

symbols.does: Materializes a generator into a list.
symbols.description: A small utility that converts an iterable (typically the generator returned by chat_parts()) into a list so the test can inspect part count, final part flags, and joined text.

symbols.does: Returns True if calling the supplied function raises an exception.
symbols.description: A predicate helper used to assert that shape_for('zzz') fails loudly. It invokes the provided callable inside a try/except and returns True when any exception is raised.

symbols.does: Probes the hardcoded resident endpoint's /models route to decide if the live test should run.
symbols.description: Attempts an HTTP GET to http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1/models with a 4-second timeout and returns True only if the response status is 200. The optional by-use section skips-with-note when this returns False.

symbols.does: A local monkeypatch of fabric.client.complete_with_tools that reports how many tools were offered.
symbols.description: Defined inside section 4b, this stub increments a hits counter, inspects the tools argument, and returns a content string that includes the offered tool count. It is used to prove both chat() and chat_parts() route through the patched module reference.

symbols.does: A local monkeypatch of fabric.client.complete_with_tools that logs the number of tools offered per call.
symbols.description: Defined inside section 5, this stub appends the length of the tools argument to offered_log and returns a generic assistant response. The resulting log is used to assert that intermediate parts receive zero tools while the final part receives the real tool set.

symbols.does: A callable that always raises RuntimeError to simulate a down model registry.
symbols.description: Defined inside section 7, this function is assigned to suite2.available_models to force _chat_context to emit a down-endpoint warning. It is used to count warnings and verify they fire exactly once per part-context assembly.

symbols.does: Counts recent store warning events whose summary contains 'chat model registry unreachable'.
symbols.description: Defined inside section 7, this helper scans store2.recent_events(4000) for events of kind 'warning' whose summary includes the expected down-registry message. The count is used to verify _chat_context is assembled once per part, not once total or twice per part.
